conclusion: 1.35M accounts is large enough for portfolio-level risk segmentation.
Matured-only outcomes prevent recent accounts from being incorrectly treated as good.
Base portfolio risk is material at 20.16%; cutoff and pricing control are required.
Total exposure proxy is the financial base for EL, stress and pricing.
Baseline EL is about 1.53B before final policy action.
Portfolio EL rate is about 7.88%, so approval policy must avoid blindly maximizing volume.
AUC is acceptable for a transparent formula-first baseline, but not strong enough for automated production approval alone.
KS confirms moderate separation between good and bad accounts.
Approves about half the matured book while keeping higher-risk accounts in review/decline.
Approved default rate drops below portfolio average.
At this cutoff the engine approves about 9.78B EAD.
Approved EL is about 511M, or 5.23% of approved EAD.
The review/decline group captures 64.07% of bad accounts.
Severe scenario EL rises to about 3.82B, showing material downside sensitivity.
Stress EL rate reaches 18.71%, requiring contingency policy.
Low-risk grade still needs about 12.55% required rate under assumptions.
Very-high-risk grade needs about 28.13%, supporting decline/mitigant action.
Formula logic has test evidence instead of only visual storytelling.
